pr.title: docs: standardize documentation suite for Temporal-native architecture

comments.body:
## Code Review

This pull request updates the documentation for the external agent integration system, including the addition of a new provider guide, updates to the integration system design, and refinements to the Jules adapter and Temporal error taxonomy. The changes clarify the canonical architecture, emphasizing the use of canonical runtime contracts and artifact-backed storage for large payloads. I have reviewed the documentation and provided feedback on minor formatting issues and terminology consistency.

The Workflow ID section implies `mm:<ulid-or-uuid>` as the representative/standard form, but several documented workflow types in this same file use different stable ID conventions in the implementation (e.g., `provider-profile-manager:<runtime_id>` and `oauth-session:<session_id>`). Consider clarifying that `mm:<uuid>` applies to execution workflows created via the executions API, while singleton/session workflows may use type-specific prefixes.

`mm_owner_type` is treated as a required Search Attribute in the backend (used for filtering and always set when creating executions), but it’s missing from the “Required Search Attributes” list here. Please add `mm_owner_type` (keyword) to the required set so the Visibility schema matches what the API/service actually depends on.
```suggestion
- `mm_owner_id` (keyword)
- `mm_owner_type` (keyword)
```

The enumerated `mm_entry` values don’t match what the current executions service/API recognize. Today `mm_entry` is derived from workflow type and the API normalization only supports `run`/`manifest` (and the service mapping uses `provider_profile`, not `provider_profile_manager`), so values like `agent_run`, `oauth_session`, and `provider_profile_manager` won’t round-trip through list/filter semantics as documented. Please align this list with the implemented allowlist (or explicitly scope these additional values as future/unsupported by the current executions APIs).

This section states that deferred one-time executions use Temporal `start_delay` and that the workflow is “not dispatched to the task queue until the delay elapses”. In the current backend start path for `MoonMind.Run`, the service intentionally does not pass `start_delay` to the Temporal start call and instead passes `scheduled_for` into the workflow (which then waits internally). Please update this doc to match the implemented behavior (or change the implementation) so operators/UI expectations about dispatch/worker load are accurate.
```suggestion
### 4.1 One-Time Deferred Execution (workflow-level delay)

Conceptually, Temporal's `start_delay` parameter on `client.start_workflow()` is the canonical way to implement one-time deferred execution. **MoonMind's current implementation does not use `start_delay`** for `MoonMind.Run` workflows. Instead, it starts the workflow immediately and passes the scheduled time into the workflow, which then waits internally until that time before doing any work.

When a deferred one-time execution is created:

- Temporal creates the workflow execution record immediately.
- The workflow is dispatched to the task queue as soon as a worker is available (there is no server-side `start_delay` deferring dispatch).
- The workflow code acquires a worker slot and then waits/sleeps until the `scheduled_for` time before executing any business logic.
- The workflow is visible in Temporal Visibility and Mission Control immediately.
- Cancellation before the scheduled time stops further work, but the Temporal execution has already been started (and may appear as `RUNNING` while idle).
- The detail page should show a "Scheduled to run at {time}" banner even though the underlying execution is already started and waiting.

#### Backend behavior

1. API validates `scheduledFor` is a valid future UTC timestamp.
2. API includes `scheduledFor` in the workflow input/payload (e.g., as part of the `MoonMind.Run` options).
3. API calls `TemporalClientAdapter.start_workflow()` **without** the `start_delay` parameter.
4. The workflow implementation's first step is to wait/sleep until `scheduledFor` before performing any activities or emitting user-visible side effects.
5. The execution record can expose `mm_state=scheduled` until the start time for UI purposes, even though Temporal sees the execution as started and idle.
6. In Mission Control, it appears in the task list with `dashboardStatus=queued` / "Scheduled" until `scheduledFor`, then transitions to the appropriate running/completed status.
```

The `/api/recurring-tasks` “Create payload” example uses top-level `workflowType` and `initialParameters`, but the actual API currently accepts `target` (a dict) plus `policy` (and uses `scheduleType`, `scopeRef`, etc.). As written, this example payload won’t validate against the FastAPI schema. Please update the payload shape to match the implemented request model, or clearly label this as a future contract not yet supported by the endpoint.

The `mm_entry` allowlist here includes values like `agent_run`, `provider_profile_manager`, and `oauth_session`, but the current executions API/service only allowlists and normalizes a smaller set (e.g., `run`/`manifest`, with provider-profile entries mapped as `provider_profile`). If the UI/query contract is meant to reflect the current `/api/executions` capabilities, these values should be aligned to what the backend can actually filter/return; otherwise, scope this section to “future/planned entries” to avoid breaking filters.

In the immediate-execution backend flow, this bullet claims the service generates a workflow ID as `mm:<ulid>`, but the current implementation generates `mm:<uuid>` (UUID4). Also, the search-attributes list shown here omits `mm_owner_type`, which is set on create and used by filtering logic. Please align these details with the implementation so the contract docs match what clients observe.

## Pull request overview Documentation standardization pass to align MoonMind’s doc suite with the current Temporal-native architecture, including workflow lifecycle/state contracts, scheduling model, and Visibility/query semantics. **Changes:** - Refreshes the normative workflow type catalog + lifecycle/state model (`mm_state`, Search Attributes, Updates/Signals). - Reframes scheduling guidance around deferred runs and recurring schedules. - Updates Visibility/query and compatibility-model documentation to reflect the intended UI/control-plane contract. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 18 out of 18 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | docs/ExternalAgents/OpenClawAgentAdapter.md | Updates OpenClaw adapter documentation to match current runtime/streaming contract expectations. | | docs/ExternalAgents/JulesTemporalExternalEventContract.md | Updates Jules external event contract documentation to match current polling/contract boundary posture. | | docs/Temporal/TemporalArchitecture.md | Aligns architecture narrative with current Temporal-native boundaries and payload discipline. | | docs/Temporal/TemporalPlatformFoundation.md | Updates platform foundation doc to reflect current Temporal-backed core and canonical queue/catalog concepts. | | docs/Temporal/WorkflowTypeCatalogAndLifecycle.md | Updates workflow type catalog + lifecycle model + Visibility schema expectations. | | docs/Temporal/WorkflowArtifactSystemDesign.md | Updates artifact system design to match current artifact/identity/linkage conventions. | | docs/Temporal/WorkflowSchedulingGuide.md | Documents deferred and recurring scheduling behavior and related API/UI flows. | | docs/Temporal/VisibilityAndUiQueryModel.md | Defines the Visibility-backed query/filter model and required Search Attribute sets. | | docs/Temporal/TaskExecutionCompatibilityModel.md | Documents task/workflow compatibility mapping and dashboard-facing fields. | | docs/Temporal/ArtifactPresentationContract.md | Documents runtime-result/artifact presentation conventions and link registry. | | docs/Tasks/TaskArchitecture.md | Updates task architecture doc to reflect Temporal-backed workflow catalog and boundaries. | | docs/UI/MissionControlArchitecture.md | Updates Mission Control mapping to Temporal-backed execution semantics and query model. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
